title: Governance & Leadership
description: Committee roles, election cycle, and code of conduct.
steps: ["President: Chairs meetings and the AGM. Public face of the club with landowners and associations. Ensures the Procedures Manual is followed.","Secretary: Records meeting notes. Manages the Google Drive and member communications. Handles official correspondence.","Treasurer: Manages the bank account, processes reimbursements, and provides a financial snapshot at each meeting. Pays insurance and association fees on time.","Safety Officer: Reviews incidents and updates site guides. Oversees club gear maintenance. Educates members on site hazards.","Election Cycle: AGM held annually. Positions elected by majority vote. Nominations to the Secretary at least 14 days before the AGM.","Quorum: 50% of committee plus one required for valid decisions. President has casting vote on ties.","Code of Conduct: Act professionally, declare conflicts of interest, resolve disputes through open discussion before escalation.","The 80/20 Rule: 80% of your time should be flying and enjoying the club, 20% on admin. If admin feels like a burden, simplify."]

title: Financial Management
description: Banking, payments, reimbursements, and annual audit.
steps: ["Four Eyes Rule: All bank transfers require one person to initiate and a second person to authorise. No single person can move money alone.","Payment Flow: (1) Invoice/receipt sent to Treasurer, (2) Treasurer confirms it was approved in a meeting, (3) uploads receipt to Drive, (4) sets up payment in online banking, (5) Authoriser reviews and approves.","Debit Card: If the club has one, set a daily limit (e.g. $200). Cardholder uploads receipt within 24 hours. Treasurer reviews monthly.","Reimbursements: Over $50 must be pre-approved by the committee. Under $50 for consumables is pre-approved. Submit by uploading the receipt photo to the Receipts folder on Google Drive and notifying the Treasurer.","Standing Authorities: Recurring expenses (insurance, TidyHQ, hosting) are pre-approved annually at the AGM. Treasurer just pays and records them.","Annual Audit: At the AGM, present a simple financial summary (opening balance, income, expenses, closing balance). Make all records available for member review.","Transparency: Any member can request to see the club's financial records at any time."]

title: Meeting Procedures
description: Monthly meetings — agenda, notes, voting, and filing.
steps: ["Schedule: Monthly, typically first Tuesday. Special meetings with 7 days' notice from the President or any two committee members.","Standard Agenda: (1) Apologies, (2) Previous Minutes, (3) Safety Report, (4) Financial Update, (5) Membership Update, (6) Site Reports, (7) Events, (8) General Business, (9) Next Meeting Date.","Google Meet Notes: Click the Gemini sparkle icon → \"Take notes for me\". After the meeting, Gemini creates a summary doc attached to the calendar event. Secretary reviews briefly for accuracy.","Privacy: Stop note-taking during sensitive discussions (e.g. disciplinary matters). Restart when done.","Voting: Simple majority. President has casting vote on ties. Quorum required (50% + 1).","Filing: Save minutes to the current year's Meetings folder on Google Drive. Name files by date: 2026-03-15_Committee_Minutes.","Communicate Decisions: Notify members of any decisions that affect them within 48 hours via the messaging group or email."]

title: Site Operations
description: Site access, daily operations, and flying protocols.
steps: ["Access Requirements: Current SAFA membership, appropriate rating for the site, and current club membership.","Check-in: Use the QR code at the launch area when arriving at a site.","Site Opening: First pilot assesses conditions, checks for hazards (fallen trees, erosion, wildlife), and reports issues to the site contact.","Wind Limits: Each site has defined wind ranges on the site guide. Green = Good, Orange = Cross, Red = Not Flyable.","Ratings: Check PG/HG ratings on each site guide. Some sites require a site introduction from an experienced local pilot.","Launching: Communicate intentions clearly. Call \"Clear to launch\" and wait for acknowledgement. Right of way to pilots already in the air.","Landing: Use designated landing areas only. Gather equipment promptly to keep the landing zone clear.","Site Closure: Committee or Safety Officer may close a site temporarily. Closed sites are marked on the website. Do not fly at closed sites.","Environmental Care: Do not disturb wildlife (especially Hooded Plovers on coastal sites). Carry out all rubbish. Leave gates as you find them."]

title: Membership & Compliance
description: Membership requirements, renewals, and code of conduct.
steps: ["Requirements: (1) Current SAFA membership with appropriate rating, (2) Current club membership via TidyHQ, (3) Valid third-party liability insurance (included with SAFA).","New Members: Welcome with a committee contact or mentor. Introduce the website and site guides. Arrange a site familiarisation flight.","Rating Verification: Verify SAFA membership and rating before a pilot flies at a club site. Pilots without appropriate ratings must not fly unsupervised.","Renewals: TidyHQ sends automatic reminders. Membership Officer follows up on lapsed memberships within 14 days.","Code of Conduct: Fly safely, respect others, follow site rules, represent the club positively. Breaches may result in warning, suspension, or expulsion.","Privacy: Member personal details are only for club administration. Only committee members with a legitimate need access the member database."]

title: New Committee Member Onboarding
description: 14-day onboarding for new committee members.
steps: ["Days 1–3 (Access): Grant Google Drive Editor access. Set up role-specific email. Add to committee messaging group. Update bank signatories if Treasurer/President. Provide password manager access.","Days 1–3 (TidyHQ): In TidyHQ Admin, add the new member to the 'Skyhigh Committee' group. For position holders, also add them to the matching position group (e.g. 'President', 'Vice President', 'Treasurer', 'Secretary'). This automatically updates the website — the committee widget and About page pull from these groups.","Days 1–3 (Website): Create an admin account for the new member in the Contact Directory. Tick the 'Committee' role. For the Safety Officer, also tick 'Safety Committee'. Verify their name appears on the public committee widget.","Days 3–7 (Context): Read the Procedures Manual (Governance and Safety sections). Review last 3 months of meeting notes. Brief on key stakeholder relationships (landowners, Parks Vic).","Days 7–14 (Operations): Physical handover of any club assets (radios, windsocks). Walk-through of key sites. 15-minute intro call with the President.","Succession Note: Every outgoing officer should maintain a Google Doc with \"3 things I wish I knew when I started\" — the cheat sheet for the next person."]

title: Equipment & Maintenance
description: Club gear management and maintenance.
steps: ["Club Equipment: Managed by the Equipment Officer. Report any damaged or missing gear immediately.","Site Equipment: Check windsocks, signs, and QR code boards during site visits. Report wear or damage to the committee.","Retired Equipment: Mark as NOT SERVICEABLE and remove from use. Do not pass on without clear disclosure.","Asset Register: See the Asset Register section for the full inventory, inspection schedules, and custodian tracking."]

title: Event Management
description: Planning and running club events.
steps: ["Proposal: Any member can propose an event. Provide type, date, location, expected attendance, and budget.","Approval: Committee must approve events using the club's name or resources at least 4 weeks beforehand.","Risk Assessment: All flying events need a written risk assessment reviewed by the Safety Officer.","Registration: List events on TidyHQ for RSVPs and payment. Events auto-appear on the website.","T-5 Days: Check forecasts, confirm site is open, notify landowner if on private land, check club gear is ready.","T-3 Days: Send announcement with location, meeting time, requirements, and RSVP link.","Event Morning: Mandatory 5-minute safety brief — current wind and trends, site hazards, landing direction, radio frequency, first aid kit location.","During Event: Maintain a sign-in/sign-out sheet. Pair novice pilots with experienced buddies.","After Event: Debrief with the committee. Note what worked and what to improve. File any incident reports. Post photos to social media and the website."]

title: Landowner & Stakeholder Agreements
description: Site access agreements with private landowners and government bodies.
steps: ["Landowner Priorities: They care about liability, communication, and impact on livestock/crops. Address all three.","Club Obligations: All pilots are insured SAFA members. Leave gates as found. Avoid crops and sensitive livestock. Carry-in, carry-out.","Landowner Rights: Right to close access at any time (spraying, muster, fire risk). Notification preferences agreed in advance.","Communication: Record club contact, landowner contact, and emergency access details (e.g. key location).","The Thank You Ritual: A bottle of wine or gift card at Christmas goes further than a formal contract. Respect over paperwork.","Insurance Certificate: Attach the club's Public Liability certificate to every agreement.","Simple Sign-off: A witnessed verbal agreement and handshake can be sufficient. A signed one-page letter is better. Avoid complex legal contracts.","Parks Victoria: Requires formal permits, cultural heritage checks, and compliance with park management plans. Use the Project Management page to track PV projects.","Annual Check-in: Contact each landowner once a year to confirm access and say thanks. Brief them on site usage if asked."]

title: Google Drive & Digital Filing
description: Folder structure, naming conventions, and housekeeping.
steps: ["Folder Structure: 01 Governance, 02 Committee Meetings, 03 Financial Records, 04 Membership, 05 Safety & Sites, 06 Assets, 07 Marketing & Photos. Year sub-folders where needed.","Naming Convention: Start file names with YYYY-MM-DD date format (e.g. 2026-03-15_Committee_Minutes).","Annual Archive: At the AGM, move last year's folders into an Archive sub-folder to keep the main view clean.","Google Workspace: Club uses admin.google.com for domain/email and Google Cloud for DNS. Credentials in the password manager.","The website's Document Management page mirrors this structure — see the Admin Manual for details."]

title: Emergency Response
description: Step-by-step response for accidents and medical incidents.
steps: ["STOP, ASSESS, ACT: Stop all flying. Assess the scene for hazards. Act only when safe.","Call 000: State it's a paragliding accident. Give exact location (GPS coordinates or What3Words from the site guide). Number of injured. Access instructions for emergency vehicles.","First Aid: If trained, provide first aid. Do NOT move the patient unless in immediate danger. Keep them warm and calm. Stabilise head/neck if spinal injury is suspected.","Ground Contact: Appoint one person to coordinate — direct ambulance to access point, keep others clear.","Notify: (1) Safety Officer, (2) Club President, (3) SAFA (for serious injuries). President handles any media or external communication.","Incident Report: Complete a SAFA incident report within 48 hours. Include what happened, conditions, witness statements, and outcome.","Post-Incident: Committee debrief within 7 days. Review site guide for any needed updates. Offer support to those involved. Update procedures if lessons are learned."]

title: Social Media & Online Presence
description: Managing the club's social media accounts.
steps: ["Social Media Officer: One person oversees all accounts. If multiple people post, one person has final oversight.","Login Security: All logins in the password manager. Enable 2FA on every account. Recovery email set to the club's official email. Change passwords when someone with access steps down.","What to Post: Event announcements, flying highlights, new member welcomes, safety reminders, scenic photos, links to website news.","What NOT to Post: Accident photos or speculation, criticism of other clubs, political or divisive content, unverified safety claims.","Photos: Get verbal consent before posting identifiable photos. Minors require parental consent. Credit photographers.","Crisis Response: If an incident occurs, post nothing until the committee agrees on a statement. Direct media enquiries to the President.","Dormant Accounts: Remove links from the website footer for platforms the club no longer actively manages."]

title: Website Management
description: Who has access, content authority, and hosting basics.
steps: ["Purpose: The website is the club's official platform — site guides, live weather, news, events, safety info, and check-ins. Live at https://www.skyhighparagliding.org.au","Hosting: The website runs on Railway (https://railway.com) — login with the club's web@skyhighparagliding.org.au account. The project is 'skyhigh' under the SkyHighParagliding organisation. Railway hosts both the Node.js web server and the PostgreSQL database. Deployment is automatic on every code push to GitHub.","Source Code: GitHub — https://github.com/SkyHighParagliding/SkyHigh — login with web@skyhighparagliding.org.au. Any code changes are pushed here and Railway deploys automatically.","Images: Stored in Cloudflare R2 — https://dash.cloudflare.com — login with web@skyhighparagliding.org.au. Bucket name: skyhigh-media.","DNS: Managed via Google Cloud DNS — https://console.cloud.google.com — Skyhigh DNS Service project. The www CNAME record points to Railway.","Email: Sent via Resend — https://resend.com — login with web@skyhighparagliding.org.au. The domain skyhighparagliding.org.au must remain verified in Resend for password resets and notifications to work.","Admin Access: Limit to committee members and authorised volunteers. Typically President, Secretary, Safety Officer, and Webmaster. Every person gets their own account — never share logins.","Removing Access: Remove admin accounts immediately when someone steps down. Don't leave inactive accounts.","Passwords: Strong (12+ characters), stored in the password manager. Hosting credentials (Railway, GitHub, Cloudflare R2, Google Cloud DNS, Resend) all under web@skyhighparagliding.org.au. Full recovery procedures in the Credential Recovery document (01_Governance folder on Drive).","Site Guides: The most critical content. Only the Safety Officer or authorised admin should edit wind directions, ratings, or hazard information.","Content Updates: Keep news and events current. Archive old content. Update the alert banner for urgent notices.","QR Codes: Maintained by the committee. Replace damaged signs. Each code links to the site's field view page.","Troubleshooting: If something breaks, check Railway logs first — Railway dashboard → skyhigh project → Deployments → View Logs. Common fixes: weather not updating → click Fetch Now in Weather Management; emails failing → check Resend dashboard; images not loading → check Cloudflare R2 CORS settings.","Refer to the Admin Manual (accessible from the Dashboard) for detailed how-to instructions for all admin features."]

title: Communication & Member Notifications
description: How the club communicates with members.
steps: ["Messaging Group (Telegram/WhatsApp): Day-to-day flying chat, conditions reports, coordination, and social chat. One committee member is group admin with a backup.","Group Etiquette: Keep it relevant to flying. No gear sales, extended weather debates, politics, or complaints (take those to the committee privately).","Safety Notices: Safety Officer posts urgent notices prefixed with SAFETY NOTICE. Also post on the website.","Email: Used for formal communications — meeting notices, AGM announcements, policy changes, insurance updates. Sent via TidyHQ for reach and record-keeping.","Website Alert Banner: For urgent club-wide announcements. Toggle on/off in the admin dashboard.","Adding/Removing Members: Add new members to the messaging group when they join. Remove when membership lapses. Review the group list each season."]

title: Privacy & Data Handling
description: How the club handles member personal information.
steps: ["What We Collect: Name, contact details, emergency contact, flying rating, SAFA number, check-in data, and event photos.","Use Only for Club Purposes: Member data is for club administration, safety, and communication only. Never for commercial purposes or sharing with third parties.","Access Control: Full member database — Membership Officer and President. Emergency contacts — Safety Officer and site duty person. Financials — Treasurer and President.","Photos: Get consent before posting identifiable photos online. Honour removal requests.","Data Retention: Keep membership records for the current year plus two previous years. Delete older records unless legally required.","Breach Response: If member data is accidentally exposed, notify affected members promptly, change any compromised passwords, and review access controls."]

title: Complaints & Conflict Resolution
description: Handling complaints and disputes fairly.
steps: ["Principle: The goal is resolution, not punishment. Most issues come from miscommunication, not malice.","Step 1 — Informal: Encourage the member to talk to the other person directly. If uncomfortable, a committee member can facilitate.","Step 2 — Raise It: If informal resolution fails, raise it with the President or Secretary in writing (email preferred for the record).","Step 3 — Acknowledge: Committee acknowledges within 7 days. Let the complainant know who is handling it and the expected timeframe.","Step 4 — Investigate: Speak to all parties separately and privately. Listen without judgement. Document findings.","Step 5 — Resolve: Committee decides on an outcome — apology, mediation, warning, or suspension. Communicate the decision to all parties.","Escalation: If unresolved, the matter can be brought to a general meeting. For serious matters (harassment, discrimination), follow SAFA's formal procedures.","Confidentiality: Keep complaint details within the committee. Do not discuss in the messaging group."]

title: Insurance & Liability
description: Insurance requirements for the club and its members.
steps: ["SAFA Membership: Every flying member must have current SAFA membership, which includes third-party liability and personal accident insurance. Non-negotiable.","What SAFA Covers: Third-party liability, personal accident, and organised club activities under SAFA rules. Check the current schedule annually.","What SAFA Does NOT Cover: Non-flying activities, equipment damage/theft, flying outside SAFA rules, commercial operations, or committee governance decisions.","Club Public Liability: The club holds its own policy covering events, meetings, site maintenance, and volunteer activities. Treasurer manages renewals.","Certificate of Currency: Keep a current copy in the Google Drive (01_Governance folder). Provide to landowners and venue operators when requested.","Volunteer Protection: Committee members acting in good faith are generally protected under incorporated association rules and the club's insurance.","If an Incident Occurs: Do not admit liability. Record the facts. Notify the insurer promptly. Follow the Emergency Response procedures.","Annual Review: Review all insurance policies at the start of each season. Confirm coverage amounts and renewal dates."]

title: Training & Mentoring
description: Supporting pilot development through mentoring and coaching.
steps: ["Club Role: The club is not a school — formal training is done by SAFA-certified instructors. The club supports development through mentoring, site knowledge, and flying companionship.","New Member Mentoring: Pair new or low-hour pilots with an experienced club member. The mentor introduces club sites, explains local conditions, and flies with them on early visits.","Site Introductions: No pilot should fly a new site without a proper introduction — site guide review plus a conversation with an experienced local pilot.","Site Ratings: Minimum ratings on each site guide are requirements, not suggestions. A Supervised pilot cannot fly an Intermediate-rated site.","Coaching Days: Organise 2–3 per season — ground handling clinics, top-landing practice, theory sessions, or guest speakers.","Buddy System: Encourage new pilots to fly with a buddy until they are confident at a site. Never pressure someone to launch.","Visiting Pilots: Same rating requirements as members. A brief site introduction from a local pilot is strongly recommended."]

title: Annual Calendar & Seasonal Planning
description: Key dates and recurring tasks through the club year.
steps: ["Calendar Document: Maintain a simple 12-month calendar in Google Drive (01_Governance folder). Review and update at the first meeting of each new committee.","Pre-Season (Sep–Oct): Review site guides. Visit sites to check windsocks, signage, and access tracks. Contact landowners. Renew insurance. Organise a coaching day. Welcome-back email to members.","Peak Season (Nov–Mar): Regular flying, events, and coaching days. Monitor site conditions. Keep the website news active. Respond to new member enquiries promptly.","End of Season (Apr–May): End-of-season social event. Send site usage summaries to landowners. Archive the season's news. Begin AGM planning.","Off-Season (Jun–Aug): Committee continues meeting. Equipment maintenance and site infrastructure repairs. Plan next season's events.","AGM (Usually Aug–Sep): Annual financial statement, committee elections, insurance review, season plan. Nominations open 14 days before.","Key Annual Tasks: Insurance renewal (check date), CAV lodgement (after AGM), TidyHQ renewal, SAFA affiliation, landowner thank-you gifts (Christmas)."]

title: Current Committee & Contacts
description: Committee roles and responsibilities — updated after each AGM.
steps: ["President: Chairs meetings, represents the club externally, makes urgent decisions between meetings, signs agreements.","Vice President: Supports the President, steps in when unavailable, often manages a specific portfolio (e.g. site development).","Secretary: Meeting notices, agendas, and minutes. Manages member database (TidyHQ). Handles correspondence and official documents.","Treasurer: Bank account, expenses, budgets. Monthly financial summaries. Insurance renewals. Processes reimbursements.","Safety Officer: Site guides, hazard info, incident reports. Can issue immediate site warnings. Liaison with SAFA on safety.","Membership Officer: New member applications and renewals via TidyHQ. Verifies SAFA membership and ratings. Welcomes new members.","Events Officer: Plans and coordinates club events. Manages TidyHQ event listings and RSVPs.","Website Sync: The committee list on the website updates automatically from TidyHQ groups. Each position holder must be in their named position group (President, Vice President, etc.) and in the Skyhigh Committee group. See the Officer Toolkit section for handover steps."]

title: Asset Register
description: Register of all club-owned equipment — what we own, where it is, who has it.
steps: ["Purpose: Know what the club owns, where it is, who's responsible, and when it was last checked. Prevents equipment loss between committee changeovers.","Format: Spreadsheet in Google Drive (06_Assets folder). Columns: Asset ID, Description, Category, Serial Number, Purchase Date, Condition (Good/Fair/Poor/Decommissioned), Location, Custodian, Last Inspection, Next Inspection Due, Notes.","Categories: Weather Stations, Radios, Site Infrastructure (windsocks, signage, QR boards), Safety Equipment, Training Equipment, Tools, Event Equipment, Digital Assets.","Inspections: Equipment Officer inspects all items at least annually. Update condition and notes in the register. Weather stations and radios should be checked each season.","Loan Register: Track items loaned to members — who has it, when borrowed, expected return date.","Annual Audit: At the AGM or start of season, physically verify all assets match the register. Flag anything missing or in poor condition.","The website also has an Asset Register page linked to a Google Sheet — see API Settings for setup."]

title: Officer Toolkit & Handover
description: System access and handover checklist when committee officers change.
steps: ["TidyHQ Groups (Critical): In TidyHQ Admin → Groups, remove the outgoing person from their position group (e.g. 'President', 'Treasurer') and add the incoming person. If they are leaving the committee entirely, also remove them from 'Skyhigh Committee'. These changes automatically update the website via webhook.","Website Admin: Create a new admin account for the incoming officer in the Contact Directory. Remove the outgoing account. Verify the committee widget shows the correct names and positions.","CAV Annual Lodgement (Secretary): Lodge the annual financial statement after the AGM at consumer.vic.gov.au/mycav. Add the incoming secretary as a delegate and remove the outgoing one. Approx $98 fee.","PO Box (Secretary): Hand over the PO Box key. Key holder details are in the password manager.","TidyHQ Admin (Secretary): Update admin accounts at Admin Dashboard → Admins and Roles. Transfer the Organisational Owner role. Do NOT remove the API user account — it runs the SAFA membership sync.","Stripe (Secretary/Treasurer): Update Stripe team members at dashboard.stripe.com → Settings → Team. Remove outgoing members.","Bank Signatories (Treasurer): Visit the bank to update signatories. Remove outgoing, add incoming. Requires ID.","Google Drive: Grant Editor access to the new officer. Remove access for outgoing members.","Railway (Webmaster): Manage team members at https://railway.com — skyhigh project → Settings → Members. Login with web@skyhighparagliding.org.au. Add incoming Webmaster, remove outgoing. The web account owns the SkyHighParagliding organisation — do NOT transfer ownership without careful planning.","GitHub Organisation (Webmaster): Manage org members at https://github.com/orgs/SkyHighParagliding/people. Login with web@skyhighparagliding.org.au. Add incoming Webmaster with Member or Maintainer role. Remove outgoing members when access is no longer needed.","Cloudflare R2 (Webmaster): Manage account access at https://dash.cloudflare.com — Manage Account → Members. Login with web@skyhighparagliding.org.au. Add incoming Webmaster, remove outgoing. Controls access to the skyhigh-media image bucket.","Google Cloud DNS (Webmaster): Manage IAM members at https://console.cloud.google.com — IAM & Admin → IAM in the Skyhigh DNS Service project. Login with web@skyhighparagliding.org.au. Add incoming Webmaster with DNS Admin role, remove outgoing members.","Resend Email (Webmaster): Manage team members at https://resend.com — Team settings. Login with web@skyhighparagliding.org.au. Add incoming Webmaster, remove outgoing. The domain skyhighparagliding.org.au must remain verified here for password resets and email notifications to work.","Password Manager: Add the new officer to Bitwarden. Remove outgoing members. Update any shared passwords they had sole access to.","Social Media: Update account access as needed. Change passwords if the outgoing person had direct access.","Handover Meeting: 30-minute call or coffee between outgoing and incoming officer to cover current issues and the \"3 things I wish I knew\" succession note."]
